# c43 pool/GMP leak-scan baseline
# Known findings on upstream master, generated by 
# The gate (scripts/test/run-leakscan.sh) fails on any finding NOT listed here.
# Format: <mode> <kind> <identifier>
#
# Triage (see __DEV/reports/REPORT-4-BUGS.md):
# - leakscan LEAK item=1645 fnXToAlphaOld GMP leak - REAL, fix on branch
# fix/longint-leak-x-to-alpha-old (not yet upstream).
# - leakscan LEAK item=1202/1203/1465/1889/2412/2413 - persistent state
# (named vars / equations / refresh), not orphan leaks.
# - keyscan LEAK seq=equation_x80 - persistent equations CLEAR ALL keeps;
# create+delete is clean.
# - CRASH item=... - handlers called out of keyboard/menu context.
#
# oomscan (matrix/vector operations under a single transient allocation failure -
# the RAM-full leak class the happy-path scans never reach). See REPORT-11 Annex A:
# the earlier "8 ops crash under OOM" entries were harness artifacts (an
# unrealistic fail-every-allocation model cascading into shared undo/display
# cleanup); a realistic one-shot injection shows the real signal is LEAKS.
# - oomscan LEAK item=1456/1457 EIGVAL/EIGVEC - leak intermediate matrices when an
#   allocation fails part-way; the upstream EIG leak fix did not free on this path.
# - oomscan LEAK item=105/1461/1535/1646/1702/1745/1751/2472 - MAGNITUDE, ENORM,
#   M.LU, M.QR, |M|, VANGLE, >DET<, vector-dist: same intermediate-leak class
#   (REPORT-11 BUG-11.1, confirmed on both plain and ASan builds).
# - oomscan CRASH item=105 - UNCONFIRMED. The plain build intermittently SIGSEGVs
#   here, but it does NOT reproduce under ASan (the authority for memory faults),
#   so it is not a confirmed bug - baselined defensively so an occasional plain-run
#   occurrence does not fail the gate. See REPORT-11 section 5.
# - leakscan LEAK item=1529/2386/2404 - matrix-editor (M.EDIT/EDIT) persistent
#   state, surfaced now that the leak scan rotates matrix/vector operands too.
# Remove a line here only when the underlying finding is fixed upstream.
